CRCL.headline: Circle Stock Soars As Bitcoin Nears $80,000 Three-Month High
CRCL.description: Circle Internet Group shares jumped 16% following Bitcoin's push toward $80,000 and a compromise on the CLARITY Act that preserves stablecoin reward programs. Other crypto-linked stocks also rallied, with Coinbase gaining 7%, BitGo rising 12%, and Galaxy Digital climbing 5%. Technically, Circle remains in 'repair mode' with mixed moving average signals despite the near-term bullish momentum.

CRCL.headline: Stock Market Today: Oil Jumps 5%, S&P 500 Drops As Iran Strikes UAE Port
CRCL.description: U.S. stocks fell Monday as an Iranian drone strike on a UAE oil facility sent Brent crude above $114 a barrel, raising inflation concerns and expectations of a potential Fed rate hike by March 2027. The S&P 500 dropped 0.5%, the Dow fell 1.0%, and the Nasdaq 100 declined 0.7%. Energy stocks rallied while transportation, logistics, and rate-sensitive sectors suffered significant losses. Defense stocks gained on Pentagon spending narratives, while software and crypto-related equities found strengt

AUR.headline: Nokia, Intel, And Sandisk Are Among Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (April 27-May 1): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
AUR.description: Ten large-cap stocks delivered strong performance during the week of April 27-May 1, driven by earnings beats and strategic announcements. Notable gainers include Atlassian (28.76%), Centene (25.68%), Twilio (27.36%), and Nokia (24.3%), with gains fueled by better-than-expected quarterly results, raised guidance, and analyst upgrades. Other top performers include Aurora Innovation, Bloom Energy, Seagate, NXP Semiconductors, Intel, and Sandisk.

TEAM.headline: Goldman Sachs Says the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Software Sell-Off Was Overdone. Here Are the Best Growth Stocks to Buy Now.
TEAM.description: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon believes the 2026 SaaS sector sell-off ('Saaspocalypse') was excessive, arguing AI will create winners and losers rather than widespread destruction. Figma and Atlassian are highlighted as well-positioned to rebound, both showing strong AI adoption, double-digit revenue growth, and attractive valuations after significant stock price declines.

SFM.headline: Why Sprouts Farmers Market Stock Rebounded This Week
SFM.description: Sprouts Farmers Market stock rallied after raising its full-year profit outlook. The organic grocer reported 4% net sales growth to $2.3 billion in Q1 2026 and opened 6 new stores, bringing its total to 483 locations. Despite comparable sales declining 1.7% due to price reductions for budget-conscious consumers, the company generated $137 million in free cash flow and returned $140 million to shareholders via buybacks. Management projects 4.5-6.5% full-year sales growth and sees potential to mor

INTC.headline: Stock Market Today, May 4: Intel Pulls Back After Rally as AI PC Push Tests New Client Leadership
INTC.description: Intel stock declined 3.94% after a powerful 2026 rally, as investors assess whether recent AI wins and leadership changes can sustain growth. The company's strong Q1 results and new client computing leadership under former Qualcomm executive Alex Katouzian have driven a 166% year-to-date rally, but upcoming quarters will test if AI PC demand and data center CPU sales can support the current valuation.

INTC.headline: The Real SpaceX Play: 5 Chip Stocks Powering the IPO Before It Launches
INTC.description: As SpaceX prepares for a $2 trillion IPO, the semiconductor companies powering its technology are already publicly traded. The article highlights five chip stocks benefiting from SpaceX's infrastructure needs and the broader shift toward agentic AI, which is increasing CPU demand alongside GPU demand. Key beneficiaries include TSMC (manufacturing backbone), Intel (CPU demand surge), AMD (CPU and GPU exposure), NVIDIA (consolidating before next growth phase), and Amkor Technology (advanced packag

INTC.headline: Why the iShares Semiconductor ETF Jumped 40% in April
INTC.description: The iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX) surged 40% in April 2026, driven by strong semiconductor demand fueled by AI adoption. Key catalysts included impressive earnings reports from Intel, TSMC, and NXP, major cloud infrastructure deals for CoreWeave, and increased capital expenditure forecasts from hyperscalers like Meta. However, the sector now trades at a high valuation with a P/E ratio of 52.3.

INTC.headline: Intel Clears Antitrust Hurdle For SambaNova Deal
INTC.description: Intel cleared an antitrust review for its SambaNova investment and continues doubling down on AI with additional funding. The stock trades near 52-week highs at $99.06 with strong 12-month gains of 391.47%, but technical indicators show overbought conditions (RSI 87.72). However, analyst consensus remains cautious with a Hold rating and $72.12 average price target. CEO-linked investments have drawn governance scrutiny.

INTC.headline: Great News: Broadcom Just Strengthened Its AI Growth Story
INTC.description: Broadcom has emerged as a hidden winner in AI infrastructure with improving revenue, margins, and contract visibility. However, the stock's significant rally has raised concerns about valuation, as the current price may already reflect substantial optimism about future growth.

INTC.headline: The Comeback Kid? Where Will Intel Be in 1 Year?
INTC.description: Intel shows signs of recovery with strong Q1 2026 results, including 7.2% year-over-year revenue growth to $13.6 billion and improved gross margins. The company's strategic pivot toward semiconductor foundry services is gaining traction, with a major manufacturing deal signed with Amazon and ongoing talks with Google. Intel is also part of a $100 billion U.S. chip factory expansion plan, suggesting the company may have more competitive strength than previously thought.

INTC.headline: Bull vs. Bear: Is Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Stock a Buy or Sell?
INTC.description: TSMC is positioned as a major beneficiary of the AI infrastructure build-out with strong technological advantages and pricing power, but faces significant risks from potential AI spending slowdowns, massive capital expenditure commitments, and increasing competition from Samsung and Intel. The stock trades at a forward P/E of 25.5x with 30-40% revenue growth.

INTC.headline: This Is My Favorite AI Chip Stock (By Far) -- and It's Not Nvidia, Intel, or Broadcom
INTC.description: Amazon's custom silicon chip business has reached a $20 billion annual revenue run rate with 40% sequential growth, positioning it as one of the top three data center chip businesses globally. CEO Andy Jassy stated the business could generate $50 billion annually if operated as a standalone company. With major customers like Anthropic and OpenAI committed to multi-gigawatt capacity and next-generation chips nearly fully subscribed, Amazon offers AI hardware exposure without pure-play chip valuat
